one_line_description: Payments and monetization platform for creators and coaching/info businesses: checkouts, subscriptions, and payouts.
auth_summary: The FanBasis API reference (apidocs.fan) documents API-key-only authentication: every request must include the key in an x-api-key header, with separate sandbox and production keys. No OAuth or other flows are documented.
access_notes: Per the docs, developers copy their API key from the API Keys section of the FanBasis dashboard — self-serve once you have a FanBasis account (which itself involves onboarding as a creator/business on the platform). A second docs portal (dev-docs.fanbasis.com) is login-gated.
api_surface_summary: REST API plus webhooks covering checkout sessions, payments/transactions, subscriptions (renewals, cancellations), customers and saved payment methods, discounts, disputes, and refunds; ~13 webhook event types. A Node checkout SDK (@fanbasis/checkout-sdk) exists on npm.
main_blocker: Small, payments-focused API from a young vendor; primary developer portal is login-gated, and getting an account (and thus a key) requires being onboarded as a FanBasis merchant.
agent_notes: Contrary to the expectation of little/no public API, FanBasis does have a public REST API reference at apidocs.fan (API-key auth, payments/subscriptions/webhooks) and a Zapier integration. Confidence is medium because the main dev-docs.fanbasis.com portal is gated, the apidocs.fan domain's official status is inferred, and there is no way to verify credential issuance without a merchant account. No MCP server found anywhere.
